for use project configurations in the test phase.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Process Recording</strong></p></td><td><p>Use the RPA Process Recorder.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Privileges for the Process Operations Module

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Privilege</strong></th><th><strong>Permitted Action</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Process Execution Plans Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Process Execution Plans</strong> view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Global Process Execution Plans View</strong></p></td><td><p>Authorization to view all processes in the <strong>Process Execution Plans</strong> view, independently of whether the current user is a member of the process team.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Process Deployment Map Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Deployment Map</strong> view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upcoming Process Changes Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Upcoming Process Changes</strong> view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upcoming Process Changes Administration</strong></p></td><td><p>Administer objects in the <strong>Upcoming Process Changes</strong> view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Finance Analysis Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Finance Analysis</strong> view with processes for which the current user is a member of the process team.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Global Finance Analysis View</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Finance Analysis</strong> view with all processes, independently of whether the current user is a member of the process team.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Finance Analysis Edit</strong></p></td><td><p>Change finance analyses.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Billing Report Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Usage Summary</strong> view.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Privileges for the Process Monitoring Module

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Privilege</strong></th><th><strong>Permitted Action</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Process Monitoring Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open all views of the <strong>Process Monitoring</strong> module.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Robot State and Operation Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Robot State and Operation</strong> view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Process Streaming Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Process Streaming</strong> view.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Privileges for the My RPA Module

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Privilege</strong></th><th><strong>Permitted Action</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Global Process Evaluation View</strong></p></td><td><p>View all processes in the views <strong>Process Evaluation</strong> and <strong>My RPA</strong> &gt; <strong>Team Backlog</strong>, regardless of whether the user belongs to the process team.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Unprocessed Task List Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Unprocessed Tasks</strong> view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Unprocessed Task List Edit</strong></p></td><td><p>Change the <strong>Unprocessed Tasks</strong> view.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Privileges of the RPA Builder Category

The **Activity Library** is managed only in the RPA Builder.

Learn how to [use the Activity Library in RPA Builder](../rpa-builder/latest/activity-library).

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Privilege</strong></th><th><strong>Permitted Action</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Builder Usage</strong></p></td><td><p>Use the RPA Builder.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Activity Library Administration</strong></p></td><td><p>Manage the <strong>Activity Library</strong> in the RPA Builder.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Activity Library Open</strong></p></td><td><p>Open the <strong>Activity Library</strong> in the RPA Builder.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# User Roles and Permissions

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Automating a process takes an interdisciplinary team. Knowledge, skills, and responsibilities are
distributed over several different persons. Not everyone can do anything or should know anything for
security reasons. These restrictions are implemented as user roles and permissions in RPA Manager.

## User Permission Scopes

Permissions determine what users of the RPA products can see and do:

- See and open
  - a module
  - a view
  - an object and its data

- Perform an action in a view
- Create objects
- Edit data
- Transition objects
- Take decisions
- Use products
- Analyze data
- Monitor
  - usage
  - performance

- Report results

## User Permissions Types

Permissions have different scopes and purposes. They can be combined.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Permission Type</strong></th><th><strong>Scope</strong></th><th><strong>Example</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Privileges</strong> / <strong>RPA (Deprecated) Permissions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Predefined fine-granular permissions to view and process data.</p></li><li><p>Can be compiled to individual roles.</p></li><li><p>For some tasks users need individual permissions or assignments additional to privileges.</p></li><li><p>Are deprecated after the migration of RPA Manager to Anypoint Access Management</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>You can guess the purpose of these example privileges from their name:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Process Automation Open</code></p></li><li><p><code>Project Management</code></p></li><li><p><code>Process Evaluation Approval to a Project Manager</code></p></li><li><p><code>Open Evaluation Templates</code></p></li><li><p><code>Builder Usage</code></p></li><li><p><code>Create Global Variables</code></p></li><li><p><code>Edit Evaluation Templates</code></p></li><li><p><code>Change Project Manager</code></p></li><li><p><code>Upcoming Process Changes Administration</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>RPA Permissions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Role-like permissions</p></li><li><p>For some tasks users need individual permissions or assignments additional to permissions.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>You can guess the purpose of these example privileges from their name:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>RPA Automations Designer</code></p></li><li><p><code>RPA Performance Analyzer</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Roles</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Combination of privileges.</p></li><li><p>Users can have more than one role.</p></li><li><p>Are deprecated after the migration of RPA Manager to Anypoint Access Management</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The role <code>Business User</code> could include the privileges <code>Activity Library Administration</code>, <code>Activity Library Open</code>, <code>Dashboard Open</code>, <code>Process Automation Open</code>, and <code>Process Recording</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Assignments</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Permissions valid for a phase in the lifecycle of a project.</p></li><li><p>Enable a user to assume responsibility for a project.</p></li><li><p>Assignments are combined with privileges.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>A user who is part of a user group marked as <code>Center of Excellence</code> also needs the privilege <code>Process Create</code> to start the automation of a process.</p></li><li><p>RPA developers need the privilege <code>Process Automation Open</code> to see which newly designed processes they can build. They need the privilege <code>Builder Usage</code> and the permission for the build phase as a team member to work on the implementation of the process in RPA Builder. If senior RPA developers need to check the build first before running tests in RPA Manager, the project manager can assign them the advanced permission <code>Release to Test</code>.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Individual Permission</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Permissions for security relevant process elements.</p></li><li><p>Granted only to selected users on element creation.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Credentials can only be used by users selected on creation.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transferred Permission</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>API Keys are assigned to users whose identity and permission set is used to complete tasks via REST API.</p></li><li><p>API Keys have an expiration date.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>API Keys are needed when an RPA Manager Invocable Configuration is included into a Composer Flow.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [RPA Manager Overview](../rpa-manager/)
- [Assign Privileges to a User](../rpa-manager/usermanagement-manage#assign-privileges-to-a-user)
- [List of Privileges by Module](automation-userrolesandpermissions-privilegesbymodule)
- [Designate User Groups As Centers of Excellence](../rpa-manager/usermanagement-assemble#designate-user-groups-as-centers-of-excellence)
- [Assign Permissions to a Process Team Member](../rpa-manager/processautomation-manage#projectdata-processteam)
- [Manage Credentials](../rpa-manager/processautomation-prepare-deployment-credential)
- [Create an API key for a User](../rpa-manager/usermanagement-connect#create-an-api-key-for-a-user).
- [Anypoint Access Management](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/)
---

# Create an RPA Project

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

An RPA project consists of a business process to be automated and a team that is responsible for the
automation.

You can create projects in the following **RPA Manager** views:

- **RPA Home**
- **My RPA** > **My Backlog**
- **My RPA** > **My Processes**
- **Process Evaluation** > **Process Evaluation**
- **Process Automation** > **Process Management**
- **Process Automation** > **Process Management** > **<Category>**

## Before You Begin

- Ask an organization administrator in Access Management to assign you the required permissions:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>RPA Permission</th><th>Deprecated RPA Permission</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Start the automation of a process.</p></td><td><p>RPA Automations Designer, RPA Automations Contributor, RPA Automations Manager, RPA Administrator</p></td><td><p>Process Automation Open, Process Create</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Create a Project From RPA Home

Create a project from the RPA Manager home page:

1.  Open the **Home** module of RPA Manager.
2.  Click the quick link **New Process or Evaluation**.
3.  Click **Process**.
4.  Complete the [**New Process** form](#process-project-data).
5.  Click **Start Automation**.

## Create a Project by Starting Automation from an Approved Evaluation

The standard way of automating a process begins with evaluating the feasibility and benefits of the
process automation in the **Process Evaluation** module. RPA Manager shows approved processes in the
backlog views of the **My RPA** module.

Learn how to start the automation of approved processes from there in topic
[Starting Automation of Approved Processes](../rpa-manager/myrpa-start).

## Create a Project From Other Views

To create a new project from any of the views listed at the beginning of this article:

1.  Open the view.
2.  Click **New Process**.
3.  Complete the [**New Process** form](#process-project-data).
4.  Click **Start Automation**.

## Process Project Data

- **Setting**:
  - **Process Owner**:

    User who proposed the process for automation via a process evaluation.

  - **Required Applications for Process**:

    Select all applications here that are required to carry out the process. All applications that
    have been created in the **Process Applications** view of the **Process Automation** module are
    displayed in the drop-down list and can be removed from the input field by clicking the **X**.
    It is important to select all required applications here to ensure that the process can only be
    implemented subsequently by robots that can use these applications.

  - **Process Team**:

    The process team is a group of employees who work together on execution of the process
    automation. Select all team members from the text selection field. Then, by selecting the
    relevant checkboxes in the table, you can authorize the team members to work on the processes in
    the various phases. By default, each team member has authorization to transfer the process from
    their own phase to the next phase. If approval for the next phase is only to be carried out by
    specific team members, then select the checkbox **Advanced Permissions** to assign
    authorizations for approval separately. All team members have read access to the project in all
    phases. The project manager is always part of the team and also always has all authorizations.
    If the checkbox **Advanced Permissions** is deselected, the values of this column are no longer
    taken into account. In that case, only that user who has authorization to work on a phase can
    transfer the Process to the next phase.

  - **Category**:

    A category describes the environment of a process in a few keywords. Choose a category from the
    list.

  - **Use Process Board**:

    Select this checkbox to use a process board. Process boards contain administrative process
    tasks. The tasks transition from **To-do** via **In progress** to **Completed**.

    If you deselect this checkbox, the previous tasks remain, but are no longer displayed and are no
    longer considered for phase transitions.

    As soon as you select the checkbox again, the tasks are displayed and taken into account again.

  - **Create automatic Phase Release Tasks**:

    Select this checkbox to create automatic phase release tasks.

    Phase changes can only be made if the corresponding task has reached the status **Completed**.

- **Process Costs**:
  - **Initial Investment for the Process Automation**:

    The initial investment for the acquisition of the MuleSoft RPA solution and the necessary
    infrastructure is required for the finance analysis.

## See Also

[Preparing Projects](../rpa-manager/processautomation-prepare-project)
---

# Glossary

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following glossary helps you understand MuleSoft RPA terminology.

## A

Action Step

The smallest components in a Workflow. Use action steps to build logical blocks and transactions in
RPA Builder.

Activity

A single unit of work in the automation project. An activity can be a bot task and its linked
workflow, or a user task.

Activity Library

A collection of assets that enables you to reuse certain activities that repeat during the
automation of business processes, such as a login, to avoid creating the same workflow multiple
times.

Activity Parameter

Map variables that you can use both in the Workflow and in the activities of the business process
and gateways.

Analysis Package

A ZIP file containing workflow run logs and error screenshots. Importing the package in RPA Builder
shows information in the same format as when the workflow executes locally.

## B

Bot Tasks

Contains the activities for the RPA bot to execute.

BPMN (Business Process Model and Notation)

A standard specification for diagrams to depict business processes. Such diagrams can be created
directly by the process owner and easily translated into a machine-executable program.

BPMN Editor

An editor that is available both inside RPA Manager and RPA Builder to modify existing BPMs.

## C

Center of Excellence (CoE)

A group of users that cover the entire range of competencies required for a specific part of the
automation. Only user groups explicitly designated as Centers of Excellence can accept process
automation tasks.

Composer Task

An action executed on a MuleSoft Composer Connector, including but not limited to read, create,
update, and delete.

## I

IDP (Intelligent Document Processing)

A technology solution that implements AI to read documents, extract data, and organize information
from different document sources.

Invocable Run Configuration

A run configuration that RPA Manager uploads to Anypoint Exchange as an invocable API to be called
by other services, such as Composer.

## M

MuleSoft Automation

Automates workflows that contain time-consuming manual tasks and integrate them with your MuleSoft
Composer flows.

## O

Optical Character Recognition (OCR)

A technology that convert images of texts into machine-encoded text.

Organization

The container for everything in your RPA Platform account.

## P

Properties

A group of values that define the settings of a specific element.

Process Candidate

A real-life business process about to be proposed for automation via process evaluation.

Project Manager

A project manager is responsible for the automation of a process across all automation phases.

## R

Regular Expression

A sequence of characters and symbols that specify a match pattern to be searched in text.

Run Configuration

A set of execution parameters and run conditions for an automated process that includes a name, run
schedule, activity parameters' starting values, and assigned user tasks and bots.

RPA (Robotic Process Automation)

Automates business processes and tasks by using bots, which helps save time and prevent introducing
human errors.

RPA Bot

A software robot that runs in an on-premises environment and executes the assigned automation. You
can manage bots from RPA Manager.

RPA API Call

An API invocation made by a customer to perform an operation, including, but not limited to lookup,
validate, create, update, or delete data in a system as part of an RPA process.

See the [Automation Credits Usage and Rates](../general/automation-credits-usage-and-rates) for
additional details.

RPA Bot Minute

The number of minutes running process automations across all bot sessions. A single bot may run
multiple parallel sessions, with RPA Bot Minutes counting for each parallel session. Multiple bots
may be configured to run the same process, with RPA Bot Minutes counting for each of these separate
bot sessions. Test runs or process runs in the test phase are also counted towards RPA Bot Minutes.

See the [Automation Credits Usage and Rates](../general/automation-credits-usage-and-rates) for
additional details.

RPA Builder

An on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the steps of the
process.

RPA Manager

The cloud-based control plane for the automations that is integrated into Anypoint Platform.

RPA Recorder

An on-premises tool that assists during the process design phase by recording all user actions,
creating click path documentation, and generating additional data to automatically create action
steps in the build phase.

## S

Secure Session

A hidden Windows session that runs invisibly and closed in a separate user account. The screen does
not show what is happening or what information is being processed.

## T

Text Modules

A set of encoded values that can map to a certain special character, a macro, a script variable, or
an environment variable.

Toolbox

The container for the action steps that you implement in RPA Builder to automate a process.

## U

User Task

An element of a process model, which cannot be processed fully automatically by a Robot, instead
requiring the support of a human.

## V

Variable

A value of a defined type that you can store and change during a process.

## W

Workflow

A set of action steps that is linked to an activity.

Workbench

The area of RPA Builder where you can visualize and modify a workflow.
---

# Hardware and Software Requirements

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Before using MuleSoft RPA, ensure that your environment meets the minimum hardware and software
requirements to use the cloud-based control plane and the on-premise RPA applications.

## RPA Manager

- Use any of the supported web browsers to access the platform:
  - Microsoft Edge
  - Google Chrome
  - Apple Safari
  - Mozilla Firefox

- Ensure you have access to your organizationâs RPA Manager instance

  The URL to access RPA Manager is `https://<tenant>.rpa.mulesoft.com`, where `<tenant>` represents
  your organizationâs instance. If you are not sure about the name of your instance, contact your
  organizationâs administrator.

## RPA Builder 6.7

### Hardware Requirements

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Minimum</th><th>Recommended</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Dual-core 2 GHz CPU (MMX/SSE compatible)</p></td><td><p>Dual-core 2 GHz CPU (MMX/SSE compatible)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4 GB of RAM</p></td><td><p>8 GB of RAM</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>30 GB of disk space</p></td><td><p>30 GB (SSD) of disk space</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Graphics Accelerator Card</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Software Requirements

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Software</th><th>Required version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Microsoft .NET Framework</p></td><td><div><div><p>4.8</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Operating System</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any of the following:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Windows 10 (64 bit)</p><div><ul><li><p>Professional</p></li><li><p>Professional for Workstations</p></li><li><p>Enterprise</p><div><p>No support for Enterprise multi-session (Windows 10 Enterprise for Virtual Desktops).</p></div></li><li><p>Enterprise LTSC</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Windows 11</p><div><ul><li><p>Professional</p></li><li><p>Professional for Workstations</p></li><li><p>Enterprise</p><div><p>No support for Enterprise multi-session (Windows 11 Enterprise for Virtual Desktops).</p></div></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Windows Server 2019</p></li><li><p>Windows Server 2022</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Java</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any of the following:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Java Runtime Environment 11 (JRE, 64-bit version)</p></li><li><p>Adoptium 11 (JRE, 64-bit version)</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### File System Permission Requirements

RPA Builder needs full write access to folders that are critical to its functioning. To avoid issues
during the setup process and subsequent RPA Builder executions, ensure that:

- You install RPA Builder as an administrator

  If you donât run the setup file as an administrator, the installation process might not finish
  successfully. If this happens, make sure you reinstall RPA Builder by running the setup file as an
  administrator.

- RPA Builder has full write access to these folders and any subfolders and files inside them:
  - `C:\Users\<USERNAME>\Documents\Mulesoft`
  - `C:\Users\Public\Documents\Mulesoft`
  - `C:\Users\<USERNAME>\AppData\Local\MuleSoft_LLC`
  - `C:\Users\<USERNAME>\AppData\Roaming\MuleSoft`
  - `C:\ProgramData\Mulesoft\RPABuilder\Cache`

- Your environment isnât blocking the execution of any RPA process

  The operating system can block the execution of certain files. Make sure that all executable
  (`.exe`) files inside the RPA Builder home directory and its subfolders are allowed to run.

#### Check for Software Interference

Some software can prevent RPA Builder from working properly. Resolve any software issues you find
before installing RPA Builder.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Software</th><th>Problem</th><th>Solution</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Microsoft OneDrive</strong></p></div><div><p><a href="https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/onedrive/online-cloud-storage">Microsoft OneDrive</a> is an application for backing up data from a computer.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When you turn on Microsoft OneDrive backup for the Documents folder on your computer, it moves this folder to a different location. When RPA Builder starts, it tries to clean up the cache folder, but OneDrive redirects this to the new location where RPA Builder doesnât have permission to write:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>From: <code>C:\Users\&lt;username&gt;\Documents\Mulesoft\Cache</code></p></li><li><p>To: <code>C:\Users\&lt;username&gt;\OneDrive\Documents\Mulesoft\Cache</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>This causes an error that you can find in the log files:</p></div><div><p><code>System.IO.IOException: Access to the path 'C:\Users\&lt;username&gt;\OneDrive\Documents\Mulesoft\Cache' is denied</code>.</p></div><div><p>A user describes this problem in detail in a <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/5247101/onedrive-remapped-my-folder-structure-please-rever?forum=msoffice-all&amp;referrer=answers">Microsoft Community thread</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If you use RPA Builder on a computer that has OneDrive, donât let OneDrive back up your Documents folder.</p></div><div><p>The <a href="https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/back-up-your-folders-with-onedrive-d61a7930-a6fb-4b95-b28a-6552e77c3057?culture=en-us&amp;country=us#ID0EDBH=Windows">Microsoft OneDrive documentation</a> explains how to exempt the Documents folder from the OneDrive backup.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

For Hardware and Software requirements for earlier RPA Builder versions, refer to the corresponding
product documentation:

- [RPA Builder 6.6 Hardware and Software Requirements](../rpa-builder/6.6/hardware-software-requirements)

## RPA Recorder 6.6

### Hardware Requirements

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Minimum</th><th>Recommended</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Dual-core 2 GHz CPU (MMX/SSE compatible)</p></td><td><p>Dual-core 3 GHz CPU (MMX/SSE compatible)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>8 GB of RAM</p></td><td><p>16 GB of RAM</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>5 GB of disk space</p></td><td><p>10 GB (SSD) of disk space</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Software Requirements

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Software</th><th>Required version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Microsoft .NET Framework</p></td><td><p>4.8</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Operating System</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any of the following:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Windows 10 (64 bit)</p><div><ul><li><p>Professional</p></li><li><p>Professional for Workstations</p></li><li><p>Enterprise</p><div><p>No support for Enterprise multi-session (Windows 10 Enterprise for Virtual Desktops).</p></div></li><li><p>Enterprise LTSC</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Windows 11</p><div><ul><li><p>Professional</p></li><li><p>Professional for Workstations</p></li><li><p>Enterprise</p><div><p>No support for Enterprise multi-session (Windows 11 Enterprise for Virtual Desktops).</p></div></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Windows Server 2019</p></li><li><p>Windows Server 2022</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Recommended System Settings

- Display resolution: Full HD 1080p (1920âÃâ1080) or higher

#### File System Permission Requirements

RPA Recorder needs full write access to folders that are critical to its functioning. To avoid
issues during the setup process and subsequent RPA Recorder executions, ensure that:

- You install RPA Recorder as an administrator

  If you donât run the setup file as an administrator, the installation process might not finish
  successfully. If this happens, ensure you reinstall RPA Recorder by running the setup file as an
  administrator.

- RPA Recorder has full write access to the following folders and any subfolders and files inside
  them:
  - `C:\Program Files\MuleSoft\RPARecorder`
  - `C:\RPARecorder`

- Your environment is not blocking the execution of any RPA process

  The operating system can block the execution of certain files. Ensure that all executable (`.exe`)
  files inside the RPA Recorder home directory and its subfolders are allowed to run.

### Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Builder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.4.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

For Hardware and Software requirements for earlier RPA Recorder versions, refer to the corresponding
product documentation:

- [RPA Recorder 6.5 Hardware and Software Requirements](../rpa-recorder/6.5/hardware-software-requirements)

## RPA Bot 1.7

### Hardware Requirements

- For optimal performance, use a dedicated computer for running the RPA Bot.
- The following table shows the minimum and the recommended hardware requirements for running one
  process:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Minimum</th><th>Recommended</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Dual-core 2 GHz CPU (MMX/SSE compatible)</p></td><td><p>Dual-core 3 GHz CPU (MMX/SSE compatible)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>8 GB of RAM</p></td><td><p>16 GB of RAM</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>30 GB of disk space</p></td><td><p>30 GB (SSD) of disk space</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

  To run more than one process in parallel, when using Secure Sessions, you must add 2 GB of RAM
  plus the RAM needed to run the process for each parallel run to these recommended values.

  For example, to run two different processes in parallel:
  - Process 1 requires one GB of RAM and runs 10 times in parallel.
  - Process 2 requires three GB of RAM and runs five times in parallel.

  In this case, use the following formula to calculate that you need a computer with a minimum of 63
  GB of RAM:

#### Unsupported Hardware Configurations

- Notebooks with Intel Graphics 520 and 530

### Software Requirements

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Software</th><th>Required version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Microsoft .NET Framework</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>4.5</p></li><li><p>4.7.2</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Operating System</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any of the following:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Windows 10 (64 bit)</p><div><ul><li><p>Professional</p></li><li><p>Professional for Workstations</p></li><li><p>Enterprise</p><div><p>No support for Enterprise multi-session (Windows 10 Enterprise for Virtual Desktops).</p></div></li><li><p>Enterprise LTSC</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Windows 11</p><div><ul><li><p>Professional</p></li><li><p>Professional for Workstations</p></li><li><p>Enterprise</p><div><p>No support for Enterprise multi-session (Windows 11 Enterprise for Virtual Desktops).</p></div></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Windows Server 2019</p></li><li><p>Windows Server 2022</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### System Settings

- Display resolution:
  - Minimum: 1280 x 1080
  - Recommended: Full HD 1080p (1920âÃâ1080) or higher

- `Custom scaling` of text and other items on the screen: 100%
- `ClearType`: turned off

#### Requirements for the Secure Session Technology

Secure Sessions are hidden Desktop sessions that run in the background and are accessible only to
the bot. An additional advantage of using Secure Sessions is that the bot can multiply itself to run
several process instances in parallel.

To use Secure Sessions you must ensure that all prerequisites are met and all responsible persons
are informed.

- You canât use Secure Sessions with any operating system running on the Microsoft Azure Virtual
  Desktop Infrastructure (VDI).
- The RPA Bot service runs under the `Local System` account per default.
  - To use another account, ensure that it has permission to run Windows services.

- The RPA Bot service runs processes under user accounts on the same computer or within the same
  domain.
  - Use dedicated accounts and a user-naming scheme to differentiate between local users and domain
    users and between human users and bot users.
  - User accounts used by the bot to run processes must belong to the `Remote Desktop Users` group
    and must have permission to establish a local RDP session via the local loopback address
    127.0.0.2.

- To enable a bot to run more than one (Windows 10, Windows 11) or two (Windows Server) Secure
  Sessions simultaneously, ensure that a terminal server software with enough licensed users is
  installed on the computer where the bot is installed. The computer must have enough resources to
  execute several processes simultaneously: 2 GB or more RAM per session.

  Although other terminal server software can work, RPA has been validated with the following
  terminal server software:
  - [Microsoft Remote Desktop Services](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/overview)
  - [TSplus MuleSoft RPA Connector](https://www.tsplus-rpa.com/)

- Before deploying a run configuration on a bot that has only one licensed Secure Session, do not
  disconnect the session, instead sign out the specified user.
  - Do not sign in to a computer where a bot with one licensed Secure Session is running a process.

#### File System Permission Requirements

RPA Bot needs full write access to some folders that are critical to its functioning. To avoid
issues during the setup process and later RPA Bot executions, ensure that:

- You install RPA Bot as an administrator

  If you donât run the setup file as an administrator, the installation process might not finish
  successfully. If this happens, ensure you reinstall RPA Bot by running the setup file as an
  administrator.

- RPA Bot has full write access to the following folders and any subfolders and files inside them:
  - `C:\Program Files\Mulesoft\RPABot`
  - `C:\ProgramData\Mulesoft\RPABot`

- Your environment is not blocking the execution of any RPA process

  The operating system can block the execution of certain files. Ensure that all executable (`.exe`)
  files inside the RPA Bot home directory and its subfolders are allowed to run.

#### Internet Requirements

RPA Bot requires a stable internet connection to poll tasks from RPA Manager. During downtime of the
service or of the region where RPA Manager runs, the bot canât receive new tasks until connectivity
is reestablished.

To communicate with RPA Manager, RPA Bot requires you to enter the following Internet route in the
**RPA Manager to connect** field on the RPA Bot Configurator pop-up:

- `https://[your subdomain].rpa.mulesoft.com`

In addition, it is also necessary to open the internally used URL `https://*.cloudfront.net`, which
is done typically by your network or security team.

See [Configuring RPA Bot](../rpa-bot/latest/configuration#common-settings) for more information
about the RPA Bot Configurator pop-up.

### Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Builder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

For Hardware and Software requirements for earlier RPA Bot versions, refer to the corresponding
product documentation:

- [RPA Bot 1.6 Hardware and Software Requirements](../rpa-bot/1.6/hardware-software-requirements)

## See Also

- [Tutorial: Create an Automation from Start to Finish](automation-tutorial-introduction)
---

# RPA Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft Robotic Process Automation (RPA) enables you to automate business processes and tasks by
using bots, which helps save time and prevent introducing human errors.

MuleSoft RPA can automate processes that require human input and can also interact with systems that
do not have an API available for consumption. RPA bots can interact with applications, extract data,
read images, and manipulate computer input faster and more accurately than humans.

MuleSoft RPA integrates with Anypoint Platform, which enables sharing and reusing automation assets
through Exchange, and with MuleSoft Composer to connect applications and data with you automation
projects.

- See
  [Create an Anypoint Platform Account](../access-management/managing-your-account#how-to-create-your-account)
  for instructions on creating your Anypoint Platform account.
- See [MuleSoft Composer: Getting Started](../composer/ms_composer_prerequisites) for instructions
  to setup and configure your Composer account.

## Use Cases

Your company can benefit in many ways by implementing RPA into their processes, either by saving
time, reducing costs, or preventing errors caused by human input.

The most common use cases for RPA include:

- Data Extraction and Data Entry

  Extract data from PDF, CSV, and other file formats and send this data to other systems.

- Payroll Processing

  Automate payroll data entry and validate the information across multiple systems.

- Price Comparisons

  Automate the monitoring of input prices and competitor prices.

- Processing Refunds

  Automate the processing of customer refund requests to make them quicker and provide the best
  experience for customers.

- Customer Information Updates

  Update customer information automatically across different systems that do not have an API and
  that are accessible only through the UI.

## RPA Lifecycle

MuleSoft provides a complete solution to manage the RPA development lifecycle, which includes the
following phases:

- Evaluation

  In this phase you use the Process Evaluation module in RPA Manager to determine if a business
  process qualifies to be automated by evaluating the costs savings and benefits of building the
  automation based on the processâs complexity, stability, and other key values.

- Design

  If the evaluation is complete and the process qualifies for automation, you can start designing
  the workflow of the automation that specifies the actions to perform and the conditions under
  which to perform those actions. RPA Manager uses the Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN) to
  create the workflows. You can design the workflow manually by using the drag and drop tools in RPA
  Manager, or automatically by using the RPA Recorder.

- Build

  In the build phase, you fill the previously-created process with specific instructions for humans
  and bots. With RPA Builder you can import a BPMN diagram generated in RPA Manager, and then drag
  and drop operations or record key inputs to specify the action steps. When you finish building the
  process, you upload it to RPA Manager.

- Test

  During the test phase, you create different testing scenarios for your automation projects to
  confirm that they work as expected. This phase works as the quality assurance step of the
  development process. In this phase, you can create real-world test scenarios using different
  parameters and can troubleshoot failed steps when there are errors.

- Deployment

  When the automation project successfully completes the testing phase, you are ready to deploy. In
  the deployment phase, you use RPA Manager to set up the production parameter values, configure the
  process execution schedule, and select the RPA bots to run the process. You can deploy one
  automation process to one or multiple RPA bots.

- Monitor

  RPA Manager provides different management and monitoring options for your deployed automations.
  You can monitor the performance of the deployed automations, configure alerts and notifications
  when there are issues, and locate the source of these issues.

## RPA Applications

MuleSoft RPA provides the following applications that enable you to manage the complete development
lifecycle of your automation projects:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Description</th><th>Features</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Manager</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>RPA Manager is the cloud-based control plane for the automations that is integrated into Anypoint Platform.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Manage user access and permissions.</p></li><li><p>Evaluate the viability and financial benefits of implementing an automation.</p></li><li><p>Design and document RPA processes.</p></li><li><p>Test and deploy automations.</p></li><li><p>Manage and monitor deployed automations.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder is the on-premise application that enables you to build the automations by specifying the steps of the process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Download and upload automation assets to RPA Manager.</p></li><li><p>Add the automation logic to the processes.</p></li><li><p>Run local tests for the automations.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Recorder</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>RPA Recorder is an on-premise tool that assists during the building phase of a process by automatically recording action steps from your local environment.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Build a BPMN diagram based on user actions.</p></li><li><p>Capture keyboard input, mouse clicks, and used applications.</p></li><li><p>Add comments and variables during the recording.</p></li><li><p>Automatically take screenshots per each mouse click.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Bot</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>RPA bots are software robots that run in an on-premise environment and execute the assigned automations. You can manage bots from RPA Manager.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Execute the automations.</p></li><li><p>Read text and images.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Hardware and Software Requirements](hardware-software-requirements)
- [Tutorial: Build an Automation from Start to Finish](automation-tutorial-introduction)
- [Use Case: Extracting Data from a Legacy System](automation-usecase-legacysystemdataextraction)

- [RPA Recorder Overview](../rpa-recorder/latest/)

- [Anypoint Exchange](../exchange/)
---

# Invoke RPA Services from Composer

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft RPA Connector for MuleSoft Composer enables you to invoke RPA processes and configure
polling triggers from your Composer flows. You can use the RPA connector to invoke an RPA process,
pass data to an RPA process and retrieve results from the RPA process.

The RPA connector for MuleSoft Composer enables you to:

- **Invoke RPA Process**

  This feature enables you to make sync and async calls to RPA processes.
  - Sync calls wait for the RPA process to finish before continuing with the Composer flow
    execution.
  - Async calls invoke an RPA process and continue the Composer flow execution while the RPA process
    executes in parallel.

- **RPA Poll Trigger**

  This feature enables you to create listeners that check the status of an specific RPA process.
  When the RPA process finishes the execution, the polling trigger starts processing the configured
  activities in the Composer flow. This functionality is useful for retrieving results from RPA
  processes that take a long time to finish and are not suitable for synchronous consumption.

Refer to the [MuleSoft RPA Connector for Composer](../composer/ms_composer_rpa_reference)
documentation for configuration and usage details.

## Before You Begin

- Before you can invoke an RPA process, you must publish it to Exchange. See
  [Create an invocable run configuration](../rpa-manager/processautomation-deploy#create-an-invocable-run-configuration)
  for instructions to create and publish an invocable RPA process.
- Using the RPA connector features in Composer requires you to connect with your MuleSoft RPA
  account by configuring the corresponding API key and RPA API URL:
  - See
    [Create an API Key for a User](../rpa-manager/usermanagement-connect#create-an-api-key-for-a-user)
    for instructions to create API Keys.
  - The RPA API URL is the RPA Manager URL with `/rpa/api/v1` appended to it. For example, if your
    RPA Manager URL is `https://myOrg.rpa.mulesoft.com`, then the value to set in the RPA API URL
    field is `https://myOrg.rpa.mulesoft.com/rpa/api/v1`.

## See Also

- [MuleSoft Composer: Getting Started](../composer/ms_composer_prerequisites)
---

# Publishing Automation Assets to Anypoint Exchange

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can connect RPA Manager with your Anypoint Platform account, which enables you to publish a
process automation to Exchange. Connecting RPA Manager with your Anypoint Platform account requires
a Connected App with the scope RPA Integrator configured in Access Management.

To publish automation assets to Exchange:

1.  [Configure a Connected App in Access Management](#configure-connected-app-rpa).
2.  Connect RPA Manager with your Anypoint Platform Account.

    See
    [Configure Anypoint Platform Credentials](../rpa-manager/organizationmanagement-settings#configure-anypoint-credentials)
    for instructions.

3.  Publish a Process Automation to Anypoint Exchange.

    See
    [Publish a Process Automation to Anypoint Exchange](../rpa-manager/processautomation-deploy#publish-automation)
    for instructions.

## Configure a Connected App in Access Management

To create a Connected App in your Anypoint Platform account:

1.  Log in to [Anypoint Platform](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/).
2.  Open **Access Management**.
3.  On the navigation panel, click **Connected Apps**.
4.  Click **Create App**.
5.  Specify a **Name** for the Connected App.
6.  In **Type**, select **App acts on its own behalf(client credentials)**.

    ![The Connected App creation window](_images/share-assets-connected-app-type.png)

7.  Click **Add Scopes**.
8.  In the filter box, type **RPA** and select **RPA Integrator** among the results.

    ![The Scopes selection window](_images/share-assets-connected-app-scopes.png)

9.  Click **Next**.
10. Select a **Business Group** and click **Review**.
11. Click **Add Scopes** to confirm.
12. Click **Save**.

### See the Client ID and Client Secret of a Connected App

After you create a Connected App, it shows in the **Connected Apps** section of Access Management,
where you can see the Connected Appâs Client ID and Client Secret.

![The Connected Apps section in Access Management](_images/share-assets-connected-app-credentials.png)

Click either **Copy ID** or **Copy Secret** to copy the corresponding value to the clipboard for
further use.

## See Also

- [Create an Anypoint Platform Account](../access-management/managing-your-account#how-to-create-your-account)
---

# RPA Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Manager instances automatically update to the latest version available; you donât need to
perform any tasks to move to the new version.

The latest versions of RPA Builder, RPA Recorder, and RPA Bot become available for download in RPA
Manager as soon as they are released and you must manually update them on your local environment.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

# RPA Bot 1.6.0 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Bot is a software robot that runs in an on-premise environment and executes the assigned
automations.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## February 21, 2024

## Whatâs New

- The System Settings panel now has a `Bot Service State` entry, which shows the current Windows
  Service state and configuration.
- The **Password** and **Current User** fields on the Session Settings panel are renamed **Default
  Session User Password** and **RPA Bot Configurator User** respectively.
- A warning message has been added to indicate that if a deployed process is running on the bot
  instance, the process may be canceled if RPA Bot is restarted as a result of a change made to the
  RPA Bot Configurator.
- When a change is made to the RPA Bot Configurator while a process is running, you can now select
  from multiple restart, exit, and cancel options to decide how you want to proceed with the process
  that is running and the changes to the RPA Bot Configurator.
- The RPA Bot Configurator now includes a Diagnostic Infos panel to help you identify current and
  potential problems with RPA Bot.
- Redundant third party assemblies have been removed.
- Dynamic process execution throttling has been added to reduce the number of times RPA Bot attempts
  to restart a failing process.
- You can now view the path to the analysis packages on the default run result message in RPA
  Manager if the setting to write an analysis packages is enabled on RPA Bot.
- You can now define the folder location for analysis packages of failed and successful workflow
  runs, using the **Custom Analysis package folder of failed workflows** and **Custom Analysis
  package folder of succeeded workflows** fields on the Session Monitoring panel of the RPA Bot
  Configurator. The folder that is selected in either field is maintained by the user.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Builder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The RPA Bot daily midnight job now executes once daily at 0:00 UT.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14807645</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Updated all copyrights to 2024.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14820545</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The RPA Bot connection test is now performed with the correct RPA Manager URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14835335</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Credential recognition is now working correctly with domain users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15019592</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Bot 1.6.2 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Bot is a software robot that runs in an on-premise environment and executes the assigned
automations.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## March 25, 2024

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The custom message created from the <strong>Write Bot Message</strong> action step now displays the full 2,000 characters and no longer truncates the message after 1,000 characters.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15252227</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Bot now removes sessions that are in a state of "Disconnected" to prevent RPA Bot from attempting to connect to and create Secure Sessions from "Disconnected" sessions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15241413</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 21, 2024

## Whatâs New

- The System Settings panel now has a `Bot Service State` entry, which shows the current Windows
  Service state and configuration.
- The **Password** and **Current User** fields on the Session Settings panel are renamed to
  **Default Session User Password** and **RPA Bot Configurator User** respectively.
- A warning message is added to indicate that if a deployed process is running on the bot instance,
  the process might cancel if RPA Bot is restarted as a result of a change made to the RPA Bot
  Configurator.
- When a change is made to the RPA Bot Configurator while a process is running, you can now select
  from multiple restart, exit, and cancel options to decide how to continue with the process that is
  running and the changes to the RPA Bot Configurator.
- The RPA Bot Configurator now includes a Diagnostic Infos panel to help you identify current and
  potential problems with RPA Bot.
- Redundant third-party assemblies are removed.
- Dynamic process execution throttling is added to reduce the number of times RPA Bot attempts to
  restart a failing process.
- You can now view the path to the analysis packages on the default run result message in RPA
  Manager if the setting to write an analysis package is enabled on RPA Bot.
- You can now define the folder location for analysis packages of failed and successful workflow
  runs, using the **Custom Analysis package folder of failed workflows** and **Custom Analysis
  package folder of succeeded workflows** fields on the Session Monitoring panel of the RPA Bot
  Configurator. The folder that is selected in either field is maintained by the user.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Builder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The RPA Bot daily midnight job now executes once daily at 0:00 UT.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14807645</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Updated all copyrights to 2024.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14820545</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The RPA Bot connection test is now performed with the correct RPA Manager URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14835335</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Credential recognition is now working correctly with domain users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15019592</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)

# RPA Bot 1.7.0 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Bot is a software robot that runs in an on-premise environment and executes the assigned
automations.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## August 19, 2024

## Whatâs New

- You can now set a display name for the RPA Bot during the installation.
- You can now configure retries for the creation of the Secure Session.
- RPA Bot now automatically detects and uses the proper RDP port.
- Added support for new scheduling types in RPA Manager.
- Updated the FreeRDP library to 3.5.1.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Builder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Secure Session creation no longer fails when the user name includes a blank space.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15456958</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Executions no longer fail with <code>NullReferenceException</code> errors when multiple sessions run in parallel.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15212084</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Starting the execution of a process on demand no longer causes a <code>NullReferenceException</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16185405</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Invocable runs no longer show state Suspended when running.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16282214</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Uninstalling RPA Bot is no longer possible if the RPA Bot Configurator is open.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15304241</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Bot 1.7.11 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Bot is a software robot that runs in an on-premise environment and executes the assigned
automations.

This version is internal only. All of the features and fixed issues for this version are in version
[1.7.14](rpa-bot-1.7.14-release-notes).

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)

# RPA Bot 1.7.8 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Bot is a software robot that runs in an on-premise environment and executes the assigned
automations.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## February 25, 2025

## Whatâs New

- Reported security vulnerabilities are now fixed
- Updated the FreeRDP library to version 3.10.3
- Updated Nuget packages to address reported security vulnerabilities

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Builder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Processes running in Secure Sessions no longer fail with error <code>There are not enough sessions free</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17427528 / W-17529095</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Updated Nuget packages to address reported security vulnerabilities.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17452040</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>An incorrect error message no longer shows in logs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17586187</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Process executions no longer fail to start with message <code>Error while trying to evaluate if applications are active</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17764023</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Remote application tests no longer fail to start the configured application.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17774289</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Error logs no longer repeat the error message every two seconds when the bot reaches the session limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17853084</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

# RPA Builder 6.6.0 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## April 29, 2024

## Whatâs New

- The new Submit Document to MuleSoft IDP action step enables you to send documents to IDP and
  process them with your published document actions.
- The new Retrieve Results from MuleSoft IDP action step enables you to retrieve the results of a
  document processed by IDP after using the Submit Document to MuleSoft IDP action step.
- The new Extract Pages From a PDF File action step enables you to extract, split, and merge PDF
  files.
- The new `ErrorOut` global variable enables you to capture error messages from action steps.

  You can select between overriding or appending error messages in the new **Error Handling** tab in
  the workflow initialization settings.

- The Read PDF File action step can now read forms and annotations.
- The Set Variable action step wizard now supports Pin variables.
- You can now specify a custom error message in the Force Error State action step.
- RPA Recorder is now integrated with RPA Builder, enabling you to start a recording from different
  sections:
  - From Web Session action steps by using the wizardâs **Start Recording** button.
  - From the App Session action step by using the wizardâs **Start Recording** button.
  - From the Workbench by using the **Record** button.
  - From the Workflow by using the **Record Into**, **Record Before**, and **Record After** context
    menu items.

- RPA Builder now includes new variable types as Activity Parameters: **Execution ID** and
  **Connected App Credentials**.
- RPA Builder now notifies you when a new version is available to download. You can download and
  install new versions from the **Help** option in the menu bar.
- The **Help** option in the menu bar now enables you to export log files, enable DEBUG level logs
  for RPA Builder and RPA Recorder, and select between appending or overwriting trace log files.
- The workflow script logfile in analysis packages now includes the current base path.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The BPMN Panel no longer shows empty when editing a Workflow in the Activity Library.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15606460</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The JSONPath Expression Builder no longer shows empty values when editing and adding new filters.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15294418</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Web Automation action steps no longer require manually scrolling to find and click buttons in forms.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15261043</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder no longer shows an incorrect error message when the login session expires.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15259497</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Downloading a process with a missing Process Owner or Project Manager no longer causes an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14899347</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The JSONPath Expression Builder no longer keeps filters from a file imported before when you import a new file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14547303</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The Check RegEx action step no longer logs the whole content of the output variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14454589</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Run Results markings in the Workbench are no longer truncated</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13902897</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

---

# RPA Builder 6.6.5 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premise application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

This version is internal only. All features and fixed issues for this version are implemented in
version [6.6.6](rpa-builder-6.6.6-release-notes).

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Builder 6.6.6 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## June 27, 2024

## Whatâs New

- The Tomcat dependency is upgraded to version 9.0.90 to address reported security vulnerabilities.
- The CefSharp.Common library is upgraded to version 126.2.70 to address reported security
  vulnerabilities.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Read PDF File no longer reads the entire PDF when you define a reading area.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16080478</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Builder 6.6.8 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## July 23, 2024

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Specifying a port in Web Session (Chrome) and Web Session (MS Edge) no longer causes an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16284120</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

# RPA Builder 6.7.0 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## August 27, 2024

## Whatâs New

- Execute macros on an Excel file with the new Execute Excel Macro action step.
- Download or export files from your Google Workspace with the new Download from Google Drive action
  step.
- Upload files to your Google Workspace with the new Upload to Google Drive action step.
- Search files or folders in your Google Workspace and retrieve their name and ID with the new
  Search in Google Drive action step.
- Mail Session (Outlook with OAuth) is renamed to Mail Session (OAuth) and now supports configuring
  a Gmail account.
- The OAuth Credentials activity parameter now supports configuring Google as a mail provider.

  See [Migrating OAuth Credentials](#migrating-oauth-credentials) for instructions to migrate your
  existing OAuth Credentials activity parameters.

- You can now configure the new Google API Service Account Credentials activity parameter to use for
  authentication with the Google Drive action steps.
- RPA Builder now uses the systemâs browser to authenticate the user.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Append to Array no longer returns an empty result when appending to arrays that exceed 10100 characters.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16194888</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder no longer crashes when you push an activity paramater into a workflow and a generated Websession activity parameter is available.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16438279</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The RPA Builder installer now links to the English version of the Adoptium OpenJDK download page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15929142</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Migrating OAuth Credentials

As part of the enhancement to Mail Session (OAuth) and the OAuth Credentials activity parameter, RPA
Builder 6.7.0 automatically detects and updates any existing OAuth Credentials and removes all
existing mappings of this activity parameter. RPA Builder appends `_updated` to migrated activity
parameters.

If your automation projects used OAuth Credentials, follow these steps after upgrading to RPA
Builder 6.7.0 to complete the migration process:

1.  In RPA Builder, locate the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters.
2.  Add the updated activity parameters to the corresponding workflows and map them to the
    corresponding Mail Session (OAuth) action steps.
3.  In RPA Manager, create a new OAuth Credential global variable in the credential pool.
4.  Map the new global variable with the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters in the
    corresponding run configurations.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Builder 6.7.1 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premise application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

This version is internal only. All features and fixed issues for this version are implemented in
version [6.7.2](rpa-builder-6.7.2-release-notes).

## See Also

- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)

# RPA Builder 6.7.10 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## April 8, 2025

## Whatâs New

- The CefSharp.Common library is upgraded to version 131.3.50 to address reported security
  vulnerabilities.
- The Tomcat dependency is upgraded to version 9.0.105 to address reported security vulnerabilities.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The copyright info no longer shows outdated.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-18213300</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder no longer shows an incorrect error message when an email address is entered in the RPA Manager URL field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-18168131</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder no longer shows a <code>Log file is used by another process</code> error when trying to export log files.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17691093</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The browser Wizard no longer fails to display pages that use a specific authorization token.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17441100</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder setup no longer fails to start due to error <code>RPA Builder is running, please close it first and run this setup again</code> when RPA Builder was closed successfully.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17699307</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Migrating OAuth Credentials

As part of the enhancement to Mail Session (OAuth) and the OAuth Credentials activity parameter, RPA
Builder 6.7.0 and later versions automatically detect and update any existing OAuth Credentials and
remove all existing mappings of this activity parameter. RPA Builder appends `_updated` to migrated
activity parameters.

If your automation projects used OAuth Credentials, follow these steps after upgrading from a
version earlier than RPA Builder 6.7.0 to complete the migration process:

1.  In RPA Builder, locate the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters.
2.  Add the updated activity parameters to the corresponding workflows and map them to the
    corresponding Mail Session (OAuth) action steps.
3.  In RPA Manager, create a new OAuth Credentials global variable in the credential pool.
4.  Map the new global variable with the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters in the
    corresponding run configurations.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Builder 6.7.12 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## July 31, 2025

## Whatâs New

- The CefSharp.Common library is upgraded to address reported security vulnerabilities.
- The Tomcat dependency is upgraded to version 9.0.106 to address reported security vulnerabilities.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder now accepts both Unix and Windows ZIP file formats for analysis packages, preventing loading errors when packages use different internal path separators.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-19076042</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The Tomcat dependency is upgraded to version 9.0.106 to address reported security vulnerabilities</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-18849660</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The OAuth login dialog no longer throws assembly loading errors when authenticating.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-19083573</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The CefSharp.Common library is upgraded to address reported security vulnerabilities.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-18748769</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Migrating OAuth Credentials

As part of the enhancement to Mail Session (OAuth) and the OAuth Credentials activity parameter, RPA
Builder 6.7.0 and later versions automatically detect and update any existing OAuth Credentials and
remove all existing mappings of this activity parameter. RPA Builder appends `_updated` to migrated
activity parameters.

If your automation projects used OAuth Credentials, follow these steps after upgrading from a
version earlier than RPA Builder 6.7.0 to complete the migration process:

1.  In RPA Builder, locate the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters.
2.  Add the updated activity parameters to the corresponding workflows and map them to the
    corresponding Mail Session (OAuth) action steps.
3.  In RPA Manager, create a new OAuth Credentials global variable in the credential pool.
4.  Map the new global variable with the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters in the
    corresponding run configurations.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)

3.  In RPA Manager, create a new OAuth Credentials global variable in the credential pool.
4.  Map the new global variable with the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters in the

# RPA Builder 6.7.16 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## March 6, 2026

## Whatâs New

- You can now enable detailed debug logging in the trace log during execution without manually
  editing the configuration.

  To enable this feature, go to **Settings Action Steps** â **Error Handling Settings**.

- The Tomcat dependency is upgraded to version 9.0.115 to address reported security vulnerabilities.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder no longer experiences memory problems from unused WorkflowRunProtocol functionality during long workflow runs (over six hours).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-21258536</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The page load strategy in the Chrome Web Session is now set correctly.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-21357835</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
