# RPA Builder 6.7.2 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## September 27, 2024

## Whatâs New

- The `StNetSh.exe` tool, part of the RPA Builder setup, now includes a digital signature to prevent
  security warnings.
- The Google Chrome driver is updated to version 130.
- The MS Edge driver is updated to version 129.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Execute Excel Macro no longer shows an incorrect error message when including it inside an Excel Session.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16603539</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Run Program no longer truncates the command line to 1024 characters.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16721834</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Read Email no longer fails to download and save mail attachments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16723360</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The logfile no longer shows entire array values to improve readability.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16760451</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Builder no longer causes an error when using Json Query inside a loop.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16766643</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Using the Exchange protocol in Mail Session no longer throws an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16793354</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Migrating OAuth Credentials

As part of the enhancement to Mail Session (OAuth) and the OAuth Credentials activity parameter, RPA
Builder 6.7.0 and later versions automatically detect and update any existing OAuth Credentials and
remove all existing mappings of this activity parameter. RPA Builder appends `_updated` to migrated
activity parameters.

If your automation projects used OAuth Credentials, follow these steps after upgrading from a
version earlier than RPA Builder 6.7.0 to complete the migration process:

1.  In RPA Builder, locate the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters.
2.  Add the updated activity parameters to the corresponding workflows and map them to the
    corresponding Mail Session (OAuth) action steps.
3.  In RPA Manager, create a new OAuth Credential global variable in the credential pool.
4.  Map the new global variable with the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters in the
    corresponding run configurations.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Builder 6.7.4 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## January 13, 2025

## Whatâs New

- Submit Document to MuleSoft IDP and Retrieve Results from MuleSoft IDP action steps now include
  these inbound variables:
  - **Action ID**
  - **Action Version**
  - **Anypoint URL**
  - **Organisation ID**

    See
    [Submit Document to MuleSoft IDP](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-mulesoft-idp-submit-document-to-mulesoft-idp#inbound-variables).

- You can now define a custom attribute to read from when using the Get Web Element Text action
  step.
- RPA Builder now shows a detailed error message if an installed service or component is missing.
- The `stc_trace.log` file now includes the OS process ID in the workflow header section.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Read Mail no longer saves attachments outside of the specified folder.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16942473</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Saving mail attachments with invalid file names no longer causes an error. RPA Builder replaces the invalid characters and saves the file with the new name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17201344</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Read From Excel File no longer parses cell values incorrectly if the value starts with the <code>n</code> character.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17246126</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Log files no longer show an incorrect display name when the Iterate Over Files action step executes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17259672</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The RPA Builder setup no longer fails to detect some Java editions during the installation process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17339515</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The AI OCR (screen-based) action step no longer shows an error message the first time you click <strong>OK</strong> in the wizard.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17395176</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Migrating OAuth Credentials

As part of the enhancement to Mail Session (OAuth) and the OAuth Credentials activity parameter, RPA
Builder 6.7.0 and later versions automatically detect and update any existing OAuth Credentials and
remove all existing mappings of this activity parameter. RPA Builder appends `_updated` to migrated
activity parameters.

If your automation projects used OAuth Credentials, follow these steps after upgrading from a
version earlier than RPA Builder 6.7.0 to complete the migration process:

1.  In RPA Builder, locate the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters.
2.  Add the updated activity parameters to the corresponding workflows and map them to the
    corresponding Mail Session (OAuth) action steps.
3.  In RPA Manager, create a new OAuth Credentials global variable in the credential pool.
4.  Map the new global variable with the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters in the
    corresponding run configurations.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Builder 6.7.5 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

This version is internal only. All features and fixed issues for this version are implemented in
version [6.7.6](rpa-builder-6.7.6-release-notes).

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Builder 6.7.6 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## January 30, 2025

## Whatâs New

- The Tomcat dependency is upgraded to version 9.0.98 to address reported security vulnerabilities.

## Migrating OAuth Credentials

As part of the enhancement to Mail Session (OAuth) and the OAuth Credentials activity parameter, RPA
Builder 6.7.0 and later versions automatically detect and update any existing OAuth Credentials and
remove all existing mappings of this activity parameter. RPA Builder appends `_updated` to migrated
activity parameters.

If your automation projects used OAuth Credentials, follow these steps after upgrading from a
version earlier than RPA Builder 6.7.0 to complete the migration process:

1.  In RPA Builder, locate the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters.
2.  Add the updated activity parameters to the corresponding workflows and map them to the
    corresponding Mail Session (OAuth) action steps.
3.  In RPA Manager, create a new OAuth Credentials global variable in the credential pool.
4.  Map the new global variable with the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters in the
    corresponding run configurations.

# RPA Builder 6.7.8 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Builder is the on-premises application that enables you to build automations by specifying the
action steps of the process.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## April 8, 2025

## Whatâs New

- The Tomcat dependency is upgraded to version 9.0.102 to address reported security vulnerabilities.
- The AutoIT version bundled with RPA Builder is upgraded to version 3.3.18.0 to address reported
  security vulnerabilities.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Recorder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RPA Bot</p></td><td><p>1.0.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Migrating OAuth Credentials

As part of the enhancement to Mail Session (OAuth) and the OAuth Credentials activity parameter, RPA
Builder 6.7.0 and later versions automatically detect and update any existing OAuth Credentials and
remove all existing mappings of this activity parameter. RPA Builder appends `_updated` to migrated
activity parameters.

If your automation projects used OAuth Credentials, follow these steps after upgrading from a
version earlier than RPA Builder 6.7.0 to complete the migration process:

1.  In RPA Builder, locate the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters.
2.  Add the updated activity parameters to the corresponding workflows and map them to the
    corresponding Mail Session (OAuth) action steps.
3.  In RPA Manager, create a new OAuth Credentials global variable in the credential pool.
4.  Map the new global variable with the updated OAuth Credentials activity parameters in the
    corresponding run configurations.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)

# RPA Manager Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Manager is the cloud-based control plane for your automation projects.

## May 20, 2025

### Whatâs New

- You can no longer publish an invocable run configuration directly to Salesforce. Use
  [Anypoint Exchange](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.flow_build_use_flows_with_mulesoft_rpa.htm&type=5)
  or [API Catalog](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.api_catalog.htm&type=5)
  instead.

  Invocable run configurations already published to Salesforce remain active. The **Published to
  Salesforce** badges are removed from all published invocable run configurations.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>You can now re-assign bots of deployed invocable run configurations.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17807695</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Process executions no longer return an error when they exceed the number of assigned sessions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-18380196</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>A new Anypoint Platform user isnât available in dropdown lists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new Anypoint Platform user must log in at least once to RPA Manager to appear in the lists of available users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833054</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A user with recently added RPA Project Manager permissions isnât available in the dropdown menu for project manager assignments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user with the RPA Project Manager permissions must log out from RPA Manager and then log in again to appear in the list of available project managers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833036</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## November 13, 2024

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Interval scheduling no longer shows incorrect times for the time zones of the logged-in user.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16739470</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Activity parameters within a run configuration are no longer blocked from editing.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16821955</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The list of run configurations in the production phase no longer sorts incorrectly when clicking the <em>Type</em> column.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16887965</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>A new Anypoint Platform user isnât available in dropdown lists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new Anypoint Platform user must log in at least once to RPA Manager to appear in the lists of available users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833054</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A user with recently added RPA Project Manager permissions isnât available in the dropdown menu for project manager assignments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user with the RPA Project Manager permissions must log out from RPA Manager and then log in again to appear in the list of available project managers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833036</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 21, 2024

### Whatâs New

- The new **Process Publish Restriction for Production Phase** permission prevents a user from
  moving a process from Testing to Production.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The <strong>Categories</strong> page in Process Management no longer shows a duplicate alert.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14436611</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The <strong>Finance Analysis</strong> view no longer shows an incorrect <strong>Process Manager</strong> assignment.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14851495</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Create Evaluation</strong> now shows an input character counter for the <strong>Name</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15301328</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Manager no longer deletes assigned applications during a phase change from <strong>Build</strong> to <strong>Test</strong> or from <strong>Test</strong> to <strong>Production</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16380363</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>You can now update multiple RPA Bots in parallel.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16382151</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>You can no longer create a <strong>Category</strong> with an empty name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16393605</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Bots can no longer receive the same task for executing the same run configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16436289</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a deactivated <strong>Alert Rule</strong> no longer reactivates it.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-16528085</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>A new Anypoint Platform user isnât available in dropdown lists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new Anypoint Platform user must log in at least once to RPA Manager to appear in the lists of available users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833054</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A user with recently added RPA Project Manager permissions isnât available in the dropdown menu for project manager assignments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user with the RPA Project Manager permissions must log out from RPA Manager and then log in again to appear in the list of available project managers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833036</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 19, 2024

### Whatâs New

- You can now configure additional types of process executions: interval trigger, cron trigger, and
  on-demand executions.

  These are supported by bots running version 1.7.0 or later.

## April 29, 2024

### Whatâs New

- RPA Manager now supports the new Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) action steps.

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>A new Anypoint Platform user is not available in dropdown lists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new Anypoint Platform user must log in at least once to RPA Manager to appear in the lists of available users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833054</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A user with recently added RPA Project Manager permissions is not available in the dropdown menu for project manager assignments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user with the RPA Project Manager permissions must log out from RPA Manager and then log in again to appear in the list of available project managers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833036</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 24, 2024

### Whatâs New

- You can now publish invocable run configurations to Salesforce and use them as invocable actions
  in Flow.

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>A new Anypoint Platform user is not available in dropdown lists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new Anypoint Platform user must log in at least once to RPA Manager to appear in the lists of available users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833054</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A user with recently added RPA Project Manager permissions is not available in the dropdown menu for project manager assignments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user with the RPA Project Manager permissions must log out from RPA Manager and then log in again to appear in the list of available project managers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833036</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 09, 2024

### Whatâs New

- The taskbar elements of a run configurationâs **Execution** are now enabled by default.
- The **Contact Support** link now redirects to the Salesforce help page.

## March 25, 2024

### Whatâs New

- RPA Manager now doesnât pause a run configuration when a process execution fails because an RPA
  Bot canât establish a secure session.
- You can now create new projects from the following views:
  - **RPA Home**
  - **My RPA** > **My Backlog**
  - **My RPA** > **My Processes**
  - **Process Evaluation** > **Process Evaluation**
  - **Process Automation** > **Process Management**
  - **Process Automation** > **Process Management** > **<Category>**

## January 31, 2024

### Whatâs New

- RPA Manager now supports automatic RPA Bot updates for bots running version 1.5.0 or later.

  When a new version of RPA Bot is available, RPA Manager shows the **Update** button in the
  **Bots** view of the **Bot Management** module, which enables you to update installed RPA Bots to
  the latest version.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>You can now edit and delete run configurations that show on the second and all following pages.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14820123</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>You can now open the <em>Test</em> tab in <strong>Deployment Map</strong> even if the project manager of one of the processes has been deleted.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14808512</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Finance Analysis</strong> no longer shows an error if the process owner of one of the processes has been deleted.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14820620</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Bot Workflow Library</strong> is now available in Japanese.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14836772</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The tooltips and descriptions on the details panel of the <strong>Bot Workflow Library</strong> now show properly.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14852032</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## December 12, 2023

### Whatâs New

- RPA Manager now implements Anypoint Audit Logging, which enables you to see a queryable history of
  actions performed with RPA objects.
- The **Usage Summary** is now grouped by process category.
- The **Usage Summary** now shows the Cumulated Bot Run Time and Average Bot Run Time additionally
  in _hh:mm:ss_ format.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The <strong>Upcoming Process Changes</strong> view no longer shows an error when selecting the table header <strong>Last edited</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14293208</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Process Run Results</strong> no longer show <strong>Endtime</strong> and <strong>Duration</strong> if the respective process is in status <em>running</em>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14311008</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>You can now download RPA Bot logs in the <strong>Bot Management</strong> when the RPA Bot is in status <em>out of service time</em>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14378109</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>You can now use the same name for global variables and credentials in different organizations. ## October 12, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The **Cumulated Used Credits** column is now removed from the usage summary.
- Global variable of type Alphanumeric is no longer limited to 200 characters.
- You can now set time-limited debug logging for RPA Bots.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The <strong>Description</strong> column of the evaluation criterion no longer disappears randomly.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14037661</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Getting the status of an execution through the RPA API no longer throws an error when the <code>executionId</code> contains special characters.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14059317</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The Japanese translation is now available for the deployment map.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14168737</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A new Anypoint Platform user is not available in dropdown lists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new Anypoint Platform user must log in at least once to RPA Manager to appear in the lists of available users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833054</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A user with recently added RPA Project Manager permissions is not available in the dropdown menu for project manager assignments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user with the RPA Project Manager permissions must log out from RPA Manager and then log in again to appear in the list of available project managers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833036</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## October 5, 2023

Changes made in this release were reverted.

## September 25, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now open the Activity library in RPA Manager.
- Process run results now show the execution ID of invocable and scheduled processes.
- Error dialogs now include a request ID to improve troubleshooting.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>You can now link Global variables in test plans in the test phase.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13980494</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Activity parameters now show properly in claimed User tasks when the User task is the first activity of a process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13999038</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Activity parameters in a test plan in the test phase no longer show an incorrect value.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14008168</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Starting an automation from a process evaluation in the menu <em>My Backlog</em> no longer shows an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14013519</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Deleted users and teams in Anypoint Access Management no longer show in RPA Manager.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13989445</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Process run statistics in Bot Management now show data only for the logged in user.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14026825</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Rejected process evaluations now show the correct state.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14048993</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A new Anypoint Platform user is not available in dropdown lists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new Anypoint Platform user must log in at least once to RPA Manager to appear in the lists of available users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833054</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A user with recently added RPA Project Manager permissions is not available in the dropdown menu for project manager assignments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user with the RPA Project Manager permissions must log out from RPA Manager and then log in again to appear in the list of available project managers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833036</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## July 26, 2023

### Whatâs New

- RPA Manager is now integrated with Anypoint Platform.

  See [Anypoint Platform Integration Overview](../anypoint-migration-overview) for details about the
  new features and benefits for the new RPA customers and how the existing RPA customers can request
  migrating their organization to Anypoint Platform.

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A new Anypoint Platform user is not available in dropdown lists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new Anypoint Platform user must log in at least once to RPA Manager to appear in the lists of available users.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833054</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>A user with recently added RPA Project Manager permissions is not available in the dropdown menu for project manager assignments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user with the RPA Project Manager permissions must log out from RPA Manager and then log in again to appear in the list of available project managers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13833036</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## July 25, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The Deployment Map now includes two different tabs to filter processes that are in the Production
  or Testing phases.
- The Deployment Map now includes improved tooltip texts and color schema for better readability.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The <strong>Send email notification to all related users when the task is ready for processing</strong> checkbox no longer shows enabled when a process does not have user tasks.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13664033</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The validation process before starting a test or deploying a run configuration now shows Error or Warning messages. RPA Manager now blocks a test or deployment when an error occurs during validation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13656062</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. ## June 12, 2023

### Whatâs New

- Process run results now show the messages of all activities that include **Write bot message**
  action steps.
- User and bot API keys are no longer visible after their creation to improve security. API keys are
  available for copy only during creation.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The descriptions of privileges now show correctly.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13477475</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Users no longer receive an error when they try to delete a credential in the Credential Pool if they are referenced to that credential via a user group only.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13505515</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 15, 2023

### Whatâs New

- RPA Manager now shows a top navigation bar.
- Improved the **Execution** tab for Run Configuration:
  - The **Run in existing desktop session as logged in user** option is now **Run in existing
    Console or RDP session if specified user is signed in**.
  - Selecting **Run in existing Console or RDP session if specified user is signed in** now requires
    you to specify a user.
  - These settings work in RPA Bots running version 1.2.0 or later. Earlier versions of RPA Bot
    ignore these settings.

- The **MuleSoft RPA Builder assignment** option from **User Management** is now the new privilege
  **Builder Usage**.
- The **Project Manager** flag of a user is now replaced by the new privilege **Project
  Management**.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Removing a user group from an unprocessed user Task no longer lead to an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13062881</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Users can no longer download a process in RPA Builder after the <strong>Builder Usage</strong> permission is revoked.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13071216</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, ensure that you import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 17, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Using alphanumeric global variables containing <code>#}</code> in their value no longer causes an endless loading screen for table views.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12636515</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The values of global variables used in a run configuration now show correctly.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12664566</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Publishing a process containing an empty BPMN to the test phase no longer causes an internal server error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12607151</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Starting an invocable process by calling the <code>PUT</code> endpoint no longer returns a <code>404 - Not found</code> error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12701176</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, ensure that you import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 13, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Manager now automatically restarts process executions carried out by an RPA Bot when the bot restarts due to a failure, such as a crash or a system reboot.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12361095</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The content of the table in <strong>Activity Parameter</strong> from the <strong>Create run configuration</strong> menu is now properly aligned.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12234671</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The content of the table in <strong>Evaluation Criteria</strong> now loads without errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12544373</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process while you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, ensure that you import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 24, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now assign credentials in the Credential Pool to users and user groups.

  RPA Manager automatically affiliates credentials that were created prior to this release with all
  users. You can edit credentials to change affiliated users.

- The new **Copy to Clipboard** button in user tasks enables you to copy activity parameter values
  to the clipboard.
- The color scheme of process boards now aligns with Salesforce style.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>You can no longer remove a process assigned to a user if that user is either the project owner or the project manager.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12193883</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Users can no longer create multiple evaluation criteria with the same name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12024864</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Long activity parameter names are now truncated to enhance their visibility.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12244827</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Users no longer see incorrect privileges assigned when logging in to RPA Manager after a different user logs out.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12421163</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Editing a process after you copy it causes RPA Builder to fail to import the copied process.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Before making any changes to the original process, ensure that you import the copy in RPA Builder and then upload the copy to RPA Manager. After uploading the copy to RPA Manager, you can modify the original process and the copy without experiencing any issues.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12472442 / W-11954705</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 01, 2023

### Fixed Issues

## January 26, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The password reset email now shows information about appending the 2FA token for users that have
  2FA enabled.
- The initial mail sent to the first administrator user of an organization now contains more
  information about their role and responsibilities.

### Fixed Issues

### Whatâs New

- Added a new Activity Parameter of type OAuth credentials.
- Now, RPA Manager explicitly declares time zones if they differ from the logged userâs time zone.

### Fixed Issues

## November 22, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Manager no longer causes a <em>Timeout</em> error in MuleSoft Composer when you use the <strong>Invoke RPA Process</strong> action to query large amounts of data.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12083939</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## November 17, 2022

### Whatâs New

- Users are now able to reset their own password.
- The color scheme, typography, and buttons changed to better align with MuleSoftâs style.
- The **Home** menu item now shows a badge that informs users about new client releases.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Users can now login using ZScaler. RPA Manager no longer fails the login attempt when ZScaler submits more than one IP address in an HTTP header.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12040207</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## October 13, 2022

### Whatâs New

- The loading time of RPA Manager web pages is improved.
- You no longer require the privilege **User Management Administration** to create an API key for
  yourself.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Manager no longer fails to upload a Workflow that has more than 10 revisions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-11808671</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## September 29, 2022

### Whatâs New

- The **Download Applications** section on the landing page is now collapsible, which enables you to
  see the **Need More Help** section without scrolling.
- The videos from the **Learning Center** now show their duration.
- The usage summary now contains a month selector. Thus, the user no longer needs to specify a time
  range with start and end dates.
- The image of an application is now limited to a maximum size of 5 MB.
- A userâs profile image is now limited to a maximum size of 5 MB.

### Known Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>A user cannot upload more than 10 revisions of a workflow.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-11808671</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 1, 2022

### Whatâs New

MuleSoft announces the release of RPA Manager, the cloud-based control plane that enables you to:

- Manage user access and permissions.
- Evaluate the viability and financial benefits of implementing an automation.
- Design and document RPA processes.
- Test and deploy automations.
- Manage and monitor deployed automations.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
---

# RPA Recorder 6.5.0 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Recorder enables you to record a business process for automation.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## November 28, 2023

## Whatâs New

- The **Retake** option is now shown as a button in a dropdown. Using the **Retake** option, you can
  delay the screen freeze for capturing image-based mouse clicks. This assists with preparing
  collapsible menus and hidden elements before the screen capture freezes. User and mouse actions
  are not recorded during the delay.
- You can now set the initial screen freeze delay directly from the image-based mouse clicks
  toolbar.
- When recording a Web Task, the selected area now has a red border.
- RPA Recorder now always generates an `RPARecorder` subfolder during installation.
- RPA Recorder now uses TLSv1.3 by default, and HTTP is now disabled for all API requests.
- When recording in Notepad++, long window titles and name attributes are now truncated in the
  Documentation section of the RPA Recording Editor.
- The Settings UI and Log files panels are updated, as well as the Manual Activity and Comment
  dialogs.

## Other Important Changes

- The attribute selection panel is no longer enabled and cannot be expanded or collapsed when
  recording image-based mouse clicks in a Web Task. After the image-based mouse clicks recording is
  finished, the panel can be opened.
- Upon completion of recording image-based mouse clicks for a Web or Bot Task, a message now appears
  to indicate recording has been resumed.
- When recording image-based mouse clicks in a Web Task, the browser window cannot be minimized, and
  the address bar and buttons are no longer enabled in the browser window.

## Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Builder</p></td><td><p>6.4.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Restarting image-based mouse clicks after cancelling the Scaling Warning dialog no longer leads to multiple toolbars.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13941631</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>When selecting the capture area on a Web Task while capturing image-based mouse clicks, the <code>Selected area cannot be null</code> error no longer occurs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13966122</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The <code>Screen capture is null</code> error no longer occurs when closing the Display Settings Warning dialog.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13980627</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>When capturing image-based mouse clicks during Web Task recording, clicks on menu items in dropdowns are now recorded.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14043830</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>When capturing image-based mouse clicks during Web Task recording, the browser window now freezes properly when selecting a dropdown menu.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14036847</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>When capturing image-based mouse clicks, pressing <em>Alt+F4</em> in the capture image step no longer leads to an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14025390</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>When capturing image-based mouse clicks, the elapsed time now matches the specified delay time.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14007202</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Changing the scaling when capturing image-based mouse clicks is now recognized.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13778082</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Recorder no longer fails to load the BPMN from RPA Manager when the width or height values are not a whole number.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13957382</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Recorder no longer crashes when attempting to open a web page that has an invalid URL containing two periods. Instead, a <code>Page is not available</code> error message displays.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-13990958</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>When recording a hover event for a Bot Task, the active window is now recorded.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12752823</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>When opening a recorded BPMN in Notepad++ in a Bot Task, HTML is now being escaped in documentation properly and only the events from the recording are documented.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14292760</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>An error message now appears when a BPMN fails to download.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14128004</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Improved the content of the error message that displays when a BPMN fails to upload.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12708752</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The BPMN is now positioned correctly when searching for an activity name in the main RPA Recorder window.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14160638</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The BPMN now loads successfully after conducting a search of the BPMN in the main RPA Recorder window.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14283085</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Clearing the cache while recording a Web Task now works properly.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14258875</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The <code>Page is not available</code> error now displays when navigating back and forward in a browser while recording a Web Task.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-12481508</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)

# RPA Recorder 6.6.0 Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

RPA Recorder enables you to record a business process for automation.

To take advantage of all new features, improvements, and bug fixes, ensure that you are using the
latest version of each RPA application.

## April 29, 2024

## Whatâs New

- You can now start RPA Recorder from RPA Builder via the Activity Workflow or by clicking the new
  **Record** option in the [Workbench Toolbar](../../rpa-builder/latest/workbench-toolbar-overview).
  RPA Builder automatically generates the workflow or single App or Web session, and you no longer
  need to generate the workflow by uploading the recording to RPA Manager and then downloading it in
  RPA Builder.
- The documentation in the Process Recording Editor is redesigned to improve the appearance and
  readability.
- Layout and styling changes are made to the user interface of the Process Recording Editor.
- When starting RPA Recorder from RPA Manager, you can now install the latest RPA Recorder version
  from the RPA Recorder home page. An **Update Available** button shows on the home page when a new
  version is available.
- You can now use the ESC key on your keyboard to cancel or close dialogs and pop-up messages in RPA
  Recorder.

### Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>RPA Builder</p></td><td><p>6.6.0 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Audio and video no longer continues to play after closing your browser when recording a Web Task.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14965350</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Recorder no longer crashes after navigating to youtube.com when recording a Web Task.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14996105</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The correct error message now shows when loading a debug file into the Process Recording Editor, informing you that the <code>jsonForBuilder</code> file format is not supported in the current mode.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15211454</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>HTML escape characters are now encoded in your recordingâs documentation and displayed in plain text format.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15099313</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Text that you copy during a Bot or Web Task recording is now truncated in your recordingâs documentation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15038064</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>The text in the <strong>Name</strong> field in the <strong>Add Activity</strong> dialog for a User Task now shows in your recordingâs documentation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-15038454</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Pressing Alt+Tab to focus the Notepad app when recording a Bot Task now selects Notepad as expected when you release the Alt key.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14607692</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Highlighting and recording events now works as expected when recording a Web Task on salesforce.com as well as on subsequent websites you go to after going to salesforce.com.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-14666842</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
